title: Affine functions with nonzero slope are injective
informal_statement: For real a, b with a nonzero, the affine function f(x) = a*x + b is injective.
informal_proof_idea: Given a*x + b = a*y + b, linarith cancels the additive constant b to get a*x = a*y; mul_left_cancel0 then cancels the nonzero factor a to conclude x = y.
formal_statement_pp: theorem linear_injective (a b : ℝ) (ha : a ≠ 0) : Function.Injective (fun x : ℝ => a * x + b)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode ea9a41a4), kernel_verified on the first accepted attempt. Tooling lesson: two earlier problem_versions of this same target timed out ('Lean invocation timed out after 60 seconds') using problem_imports=["Mathlib"] (the full library) -- twice, deterministically, regardless of proof_term -- while problem_imports=["Mathlib.Tactic.Linarith"] alone elaborated fast but failed with missing real-number typeclass instances (OfNat/Mul on ℝ), since the tactic import alone doesn't pull in the real-number algebraic structure. The combination that worked: ["Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic", "Mathlib.Tactic.Linarith"] -- narrow and fast. Prefer narrow, targeted problem_imports over the blanket ["Mathlib"] for real-number targets in this environment; reserve full Mathlib import for cases where the narrow set genuinely can't be identified. Closes the general (parametrized a != 0) linear_injective item in packets/elementary/functions/QUEUE.md, distinct from the already-landed linear_injective_concrete_instance packet.
